title: sambucus nigra f. albovariegata
intro: Sambucus nigra f. albovariegata, also known as the white-variegated elderberry, is a flowering plant that belongs to the Adoxaceae family. The medicinal properties of this plant are attributed to its berries, flowers, and roots, which contain bioactive compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of using Sambucus nigra f. albovariegata include its potential to alleviate cold and flu symptoms, reduce fever, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to make elderberry syrup, a popular remedy for cold and flu relief.
